name: Fifth District Savings Bank
description_short: Fifth District Savings Bank is a community bank founded in 1926 serving the New Orleans area with checking, savings, debit cards, residential loans, and FDIC-insured deposits.
description_long:
Fifth District Savings Bank has been operating as one of the oldest community banks in the New Orleans area since its founding in 1926, making it a century-old financial institution with deep local roots. The bank serves its community with a focus on personalized, local service rather than national chain banking. They emphasize their status as a community bank with local lenders available to assist customers through financial decisions.

The bank offers a range of retail banking services including free checking accounts, savings accounts with competitive rates, debit cards with chip technology, residential loans (purchase, construction, and renovation), online banking, loan payment services, and card statement access. They provide FDIC insurance protection on deposits up to maximum allowed limits and actively promote rate calculators and coverage calculators to help customers understand their financial options.

Fifth District distinguishes itself primarily through its community-oriented positioning and longevity—celebrating 100 years of operation. They emphasize local decision-making, personal relationships with lenders, and a commitment to knowing their customers. The bank also provides employee benefits including 401K with profit sharing, ESOP participation, tuition reimbursement, and health/dental/vision insurance, suggesting operational stability and investment in workforce retention.

As a traditional community bank, Fifth District serves customers seeking local banking relationships and personalized service rather than digital-first or fintech solutions. However, the website provides limited detail on specific product features, rates, fees, or competitive advantages beyond the community bank positioning. Prospective customers should compare their specific rates and loan terms against regional and national competitors before making decisions.
services: Free checking accounts with promotional gifts
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. government backing, up to maximum coverage limits
Free checking account with promotional gifts for opening and referrals
100 years of operational history since 1926, indicating stability and local market knowledge
Local lenders available for residential loans (purchase, build, renovation) with personalized guidance
Debit cards include chip technology for fraud protection
Competitive savings rates advertised on homepage
Comprehensive employee benefits (401K with profit sharing, tuition reimbursement, ESOP) suggest institutional stability
cons: Website provides limited information on specific interest rates, APYs, or fee structures
Residential loan products appear to be the primary credit offering; personal loans not prominently mentioned
Limited online presence details—unclear full scope of digital banking capabilities beyond login
No information on credit cards, lines of credit, or alternative lending products
Geographic limitation to New Orleans area may restrict accessibility for out-of-region customers

contact.address: 4000 General Degaulle Dr, New Orleans, LA 70114

best_for: New Orleans-area residents seeking traditional community banking with local decision-making
Home buyers or property owners interested in residential loans with personalized lender relationships
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance protection and established institutional stability
People who value in-person banking relationships over digital-only financial services
diagnosis: Fifth District Savings Bank is best for New Orleans-area customers seeking a traditional community banking experience with local relationships, FDIC-insured deposits, and residential lending. The primary caveat is the limited product information available online and apparent geographic restriction to the New Orleans region, plus lack of competitive detail on rates and fees compared to regional and national alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 4000 General Degaulle Dr, New Orleans, LA 70114
